knowledge.insight: Johnston: On the St. Lawrence River, sonar doesn't always mark bass on drop-offs because river bass are tight to the bottom. An underwater camera with a trolling fin and extra weight lets you get down to depth and get a stable picture of what's happening.

Johnston: Drop-offs are key spots against which bass can push bait. Use an HD underwater camera with on-screen water temperature and depth to verify fish presence on these spots when sonar can't distinguish bottom-hugging bass.
